content.full_text:
July 24, 1992


Fair Political Practices Commission
Post Office Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804

Dear Commissioners:

I was elected Mayor of the City of Roseville in November of 1989. Within a few months, my election committee was closed and all business correspondence completed.

In 1992, I received a letter from the Sacramento Bee Neighbors stating I had a $204.46 credit. My former treasurer and I closed our Bee account 3 years earlier and was surprised to receive this notice. I called the paper and was informed the credit could be taken in check form.

What is the proper procedure for me to follow regarding reporting the recent information on the advertising credit.

Sincerely,

Pauline Roccucci, Councilmember
City of Roseville

PR/mb

sections.question: What is the proper procedure for me to follow regarding reporting the recent information on the advertising credit?
sections.question_synthetic: A former mayoral candidate who closed her election committee three years ago has received an unexpected advertising credit from a newspaper and is seeking guidance on how to properly report this credit.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter to FPPC, not an FPPC response. No conclusion or advice section present. Letter appears incomplete or partially scanned (ends with '?' and 'PR/mb' notation). Question clearly stated at end of letter body.

embedding.qa_text: QUESTION: What is the proper procedure for me to follow regarding reporting the recent information on the advertising credit?

My former treasurer and I closed our Bee account 3 years earlier and was surprised to receive this notice. I called the paper and was informed the credit could be taken in check form. What is the proper procedure for me to follow regarding reporting the recent information on the advertising credit. Sincerely, Pauline Roccucci, Councilmember City of Roseville PR/mb
embedding.summary: Incoming letter from Pauline Roccucci, Councilmember of Roseville, requesting FPPC guidance on proper reporting procedures for an unexpected $204.46 advertising credit from the Sacramento Bee, nearly three years after her election committee was closed.
